columns.description: ACTUAL SPECIFIC columns for tracking the entities the user wants to research (e.g., if tracking AI companies: 'Company Name', 'Website', 'Is Hiring for AI?'). DO NOT create meta-columns like 'Table Name', 'Column Name', 'Description', 'Format' - those are for describing tables, not for tracking the actual entities.

name.description: SPECIFIC column name for the entity being tracked (e.g., 'Company Name', 'Paper Title', 'Product Name') - NOT generic like 'Entity Name' or 'Table Name'

description.description: What data this SPECIFIC column contains for the entity (e.g., 'Name of the AI company', 'URL of company website')

format.description: Data format (String, Number, Boolean, URL, Date, etc.)

importance.description: Column importance level. Use 'ID' for identification columns (simple identifiers like names, dates, URLs). Use 'RESEARCH' for columns requiring validation.

validation_strategy.description: How to validate this column's data (required for research columns)

search_strategy.description: Search strategy with requirements (always required) and subdomains (required only for row discovery path, not needed for complete_rows path)

description.description: Clear description of what entities to find

requirements.description: Row requirements (minimum 1) - can be hard (dealbreakers) or soft (preferences). Bias toward soft requirements.

type.description: Hard = absolute dealbreaker, Soft = preference that improves scores

requirements_notes.description: Overall guidance about what makes a good row

default_included_domains.description: Optional: Default domains to focus on for searches (authoritative sources)

default_excluded_domains.description: Optional: Default domains to exclude from searches (defaults: youtube.com, reddit.com unless specifically needed)

subdomains.description: 🚨 MANDATORY when trigger_row_discovery=true, FORBIDDEN when false. 1-10 subdomains for parallel row discovery with search queries and targets. Use 1 subdomain when targeting a single specific entity type. Use 2+ for broader discovery. EXECUTION WILL FAIL if trigger_row_discovery=true and this is missing/empty.

focus.description: Detailed description of this subdomain's focus area

search_queries.description: Search queries specific to this subdomain that yield MULTIPLE entities

target_rows.description: Number of candidate rows to find for this subdomain (allows small allocations for niche subdomains)

discovered_list_url.description: Optional: URL of authoritative list/database where candidates can be found (e.g., NIH RePORTER, Wikipedia list, Crunchbase). Leave empty if no single authoritative source exists.

candidates.description: Optional: 3-10 actual example candidates discovered from authoritative lists, with their primary ID values (e.g., ['Anthropic', 'OpenAI'] for companies or ['John Andrews - USC', 'Sarah Davis - MIT'] for researchers). Must be REAL entities found via web search, not made-up examples.

included_domains.description: Optional: Override default included domains for this specific subdomain

excluded_domains.description: Optional: Override default excluded domains for this specific subdomain

prepopulated_rows_markdown.description:
Markdown table with prepopulated rows. Use numbered citations [1] [2] after each cell value to indicate source. Example: | Company | Revenue |
|---------|--------|
| Walmart[1] | $680B[2] |
| Amazon[1] | $574B[2] |

Include ALL rows you can extract from available sources. Each value should have a citation number in brackets.

citations.description: Map of citation numbers to source URLs. Example: {'1': 'https://fortune.com/ranking/fortune500/2024/', '2': 'https://fortune.com/ranking/fortune500/?stream=top'}

trigger_row_discovery.description: 🚨 ANSWER THE 3 QUESTIONS AT TOP OF PROMPT FIRST! If all YES → set FALSE. If any NO → set TRUE. FALSE = you have what user wants with good data (provide skip_rationale, NO subdomains). TRUE = need web search for more rows (provide discovery_guidance, MUST include subdomains array 1-10 items or schema validation will FAIL). When unsure: choose FALSE (safer).

skip_rationale.description: REQUIRED ONLY when trigger_row_discovery=false, MUST be omitted when true. Explain why row discovery is not needed (e.g., 'Generated complete set of 50 US states from model knowledge', 'Extracted all 54 references from conversation', 'Step 0b provided complete table with all requested data')

discovery_guidance.description: REQUIRED ONLY when trigger_row_discovery=true, MUST be omitted when false. Guide row discovery on what to focus on (e.g., 'Need to populate Employee Count and Has Job Posting columns for all rows', 'Need to find 20 more companies beyond the 30 provided', 'Focus on finding smaller startups not in the Forbes list')
